text:
"A Rickle in Time" is the first episode of the second season of Rick and Morty. It is the twelfth episode of the series overall. It premiered on July 26, 2015. It was written by Matt Roller and directed by Wes Archer. The episode is rated TV-14-L.

Rick unfreezes time, but since he, Morty and Summer have been living for so long in frozen time, their time fractures into multiple possible eventualities.

The episode opens with time still frozen from the ending of Season One. Outside of the Smith Residence, Morty and Summer finish up some last minute cleaning duties while Jerry and Beth are still frozen outside. It's revealed that time has been frozen for nearly six months. Before Rick unfreezes time, he admits to Morty and Summer that 'their' time may be unstable for a while, and as a precaution, he tells Morty and Summer not to touch their parents at all, or their time might "break."

When time is unfrozen, Mr. Benson falls off the roof while both Jerry and Beth are very clearly confused with the entire house not destroyed. Rick insists that Jerry was just seeing what he wanted to see due to his negative attitude, and he warmly greets Beth. Beth attempts to hug her father and children, but they pull back, insisting it's because they missed them a lot and hugging would destroy them (it literally would). As a means to play it safe, Rick bribes the two into leaving to buy ice cream. He kicks a pile of unmarked bills to get them out of the house. Soon, Morty and Summer experience a bit of sibling rivalry over who's closer to Rick. As they shove each other, the uncertainty causes their time to split, creating two parallel versions of their reality which exist separately from each other. Rick says that their uncertainty has broken their existence into "two equally possible impossibilities." Morty suggests theres supposed to be infinite timelines, but Rick says they aren't in any timeline, and to prove it he opens the garage door to reveal the house floating in the middle of a black void surrounded by Schrödinger's cats.

After getting ice cream, Jerry and Beth accidentally strike a deer. The deer isn't killed but severely injured. Beth wants to help it but is hesitant because they are in the middle of the woods rather than at her horse hospital. Jerry tries to comfort her, but slips up and says something that hurts Beth's ego, causing Beth to take it upon herself to save the deer no matter what. Beth takes the deer into a veterinary hospital and attempts to perform surgery. Before she begins her procedure, she notices a gunshot wound in its side. A hunter appears and says he was the one who shot him. He claims the deer belongs to him and calls his lawyer to help him gain ownership. The hunter's lawyer appears at the animal hospital and lets them know about Brad's Law and how legally the deer belongs to the hunter. Beth ignores him and continues to operate.

Rick uses a Time Crystal to try to mend the timelines together, but Morty and Summer's continued uncertainty prevents the fusion from working. Rick demands to know the reason behind their uncertainty. He deduces that the two of them 'want to be grandpa's favorite' and then sits them down in front of a whiteboard and cruelly tells both of them how little he thinks of them, and how they are no different from each other in his eyes. His long speech clearly upsets both of them. Before Rick attempts to mend time together once again, the Morty on one side of the split makes a quip about his drinking habits which causes that rick to be delayed by a few seconds in pushing the button, resulting in the two Ricks pushing their buttons out of sync. The time fusion fails for the second time and both Ricks immediately think that their 'other self' is trying to kill him, saying it would be easier to eliminate one of the possibilities, rather than fuse them back together.

Rick quickly creates a pistol with his time crystal and attempts to kill the other self. A shootout opens up with both Ricks across timelines trying to murder each other. The chaos results in time splitting a second time, leading to four separate realities. Morty knocks out Rick from behind in order to calm him down. Morty and Summer put Rick into a dog crate. Rick reawakens in the crate and proves he's certain across all four timelines by calling himself and leaving a message. He then receives three other voicemails simultaneously. Suddenly, a Fourth Dimensional Being appears and scolds Rick for attempting to piece time together with just a crystal. He gives Rick, Morty, and Summer time-stabilizing collars that then correctly fuse their times back to its regular place. However, since Rick obtained the time crystal illegally, the being tells them that they will go to Time Prison for eternity.

After the being calls for backup, Rick tricks him and makes a grab for his 'gun' Chris, but gets grossed out and drops it. It wriggles away into the street, where it is run over by a vehicle and killed. Rick orders Mor
